pages.full_text:
Sara, Jones. Good. No choice, hard boiled eggs, cold leeks, pasta corkscrews, roast pork, cheese, icecreams + coffee £40. 18 men + 3 women in restaurant.
   We napped + Senon, Thomas, Peter, John + Charles paddled in the river. Men are fishing in the river from little boats. It's green + woody. Lots of little fish. That evening Thomas, William + Peter beat Eve at ping pong.
   A cavalcade of vans + caravans arrived, complete with a washing machine + satelite dish.
**** Wed Aug 2nd
   We left + went to the bee museum at [illegible] . We saw the old equipment - a pottery smoker similar to those used by Greeks + Romans. We bought bee creams at the shop + mead, having seen the mead cellars, but failed to see the out door apiary. The mead takes 18 ms. in the processes + 5 years to mature.
   We had hoped to canoe in a campsite at Arcis sur Aube, but it was shut + overcrowded, so we lunched there + drove south to St. Remy on the old road from Arcis - Troyes. It's a green, quiet campsite with a barn with ping pong + football. In the afternoon we went down to the river to paddle. It's like the Itchen, a
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread mid-sentence. 'Sara, Jones' likely refers to a restaurant or host. 'Senon' possibly a place name for canoeing/river location. 'Arcis sur Aube' is a town in the Aube department, France; the route Arcis-Troyes is consistent with Champagne region. The year is uncertain — 'Wed Aug 2nd' matches 1995, 2000, or 2006; volume covers 1989-2003, so most likely 1995 or 2000. Confidence is low without more context. The bee museum location name is illegible.

pages.full_text:
chalky river. Charles is very brave with the cold water. Everyone took it in turns to ice down in the freezing water. White chalk billows up. We camped at St. Remy/Noue***, on the brow of a hill, a cool breeze. We camped under plum tree with delicious green plums the owner came us eating them at supper time. It was difficult to get Charles out of the sandpit. A pingpong table + football table were in a barn. It was cheap, very much a Transit camp. It was almost empty when we arrived at tea time (when Thomas tipped scalding tea over me) and cheek-by-jowl at bedtime. More people had left by the time we did at 9.30. There were many British.
   We walked around Troyes. The cathedral is lovely, calm, uncluttered. Stained glass windows. Troyes grew rich from being on the Italy/Belgium trade route. The houses have timber frames, vertical/horizontal, not diagonal, with brick infill.
[sketch of house facade] The floors of houses, almost meet on the 3rd or [sketch of house facade] 4th storey. The endos of beams is sometimes carved. We had coffee. Lunch was in a wood, then we stopped at a supermarket by a canal.
   On to [?Arcy]-sur-[?Cure]**** There is a fast flowing river, where we had a good time swimmin[g]
pages.notes: Small pen sketches of timber-framed house facades appear inline with the text (not a route map). Last place name reads 'Arcis-sur-Cure' or '[?Arcy]-sur-[?Cure]' — Arcy-sur-Cure (Yonne, Burgundy) is a real village with caves and a river, which fits the description of a fast-flowing river for swimming; this is the more likely reading. Text is cut off at the bottom of the image mid-word ('swimmin[g]').
